macro_goal: compose long movement bursts with periodic control switches until the correspondence state advances again
level_hypotheses: level 3 relies on chained bursts of ACTION2 and ACTION1 rather than a single primitive
ACTION5 still matters as a control switch but should be used sparingly between movement bursts
ACTION3 and ACTION4 are secondary reposition moves, not the main driver of progress
the final push into the next level is usually completed by an ACTION2 burst

entities.name: gray_shape
entities.notes: changes which correspondence branch is currently active

entities.name: purple_shape
entities.notes: match target for the active correspondence state

win_condition_hypotheses: alternate horizontal and vertical movement bursts until a valid correspondence is created
late-stage progress is usually sealed by repeated ACTION2 after a switch or reposition

constraints.description: ACTION5 continues to switch which branch the next movement burst affects
single isolated moves are weak; useful progress comes from short repeated bursts

anti_patterns: do not waste the level budget on long pure probing once ACTION1, ACTION2, and ACTION5 are already grounded
do not overuse ACTION5 without following it with a movement burst

subgoal_tests.description: confirm ACTION1 can drive a sustained movement burst on level 3
subgoal_tests.verification: repeated ACTION1 moves the active shape along a stable lane

subgoal_tests.description: confirm ACTION2 remains the best finishing burst on level 3
subgoal_tests.verification: repeated ACTION2 advances the active shape through the corridor

subgoal_tests.description: alternate ACTION1 and ACTION2 bursts, inserting ACTION5 only when needed to swap the active branch, until the correspondence level advances
subgoal_tests.verification: level advances after a burst-switch-burst composition
